description: Captures video from PoE camera via RTSP, applies quality gate, and delivers to ORC-OS incoming directory. Managed by systemd timer.
readme:
This service wraps /usr/local/bin/orc-capture, which handles relay control, camera config enforcement, RTSP capture, and quality gating.

## Configuration files

- **/etc/orc-capture.conf** — Camera IP, RTSP path, capture duration, video codec, quality gate thresholds, and output directory. Edit this file to change camera settings or capture behavior.
- **~/.ORC-OS/services/orc-capture.timer** — Timer frequency (default: every 15 minutes). Edit the OnCalendar line, then run: sudo systemctl daemon-reload && sudo systemctl restart orc-capture.timer
- **/usr/local/bin/orc-capture** — The capture script itself. Deployed via deploy.sh from the repo.

## Timer frequency

The default capture interval is 15 minutes (fires at :00, :15, :30, :45). To change it, edit the timer file:

    sudo nano ~/.ORC-OS/services/orc-capture.timer

Change OnCalendar=*:0/15 to your desired interval (e.g. *:0/5 for every 5 minutes). Then reload:

    sudo systemctl daemon-reload
    sudo systemctl restart orc-capture.timer

parameters.description: Master enable for capture. When disabled (0), the timer still fires but the script exits immediately.

script_content:
#!/bin/bash
# ORC Video Capture — thin wrapper for ORC-OS service management
# Actual capture logic lives in /usr/local/bin/orc-capture
# Runtime config lives in /etc/orc-capture.conf

if [ "${CAPTURE_ENABLED}" = "0" ]; then
    echo "$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S') [orc-capture] Capture disabled via ORC-OS, skipping"
    exit 0
fi

exec /usr/local/bin/orc-capture
